result:
I want to talk about how fucking beautiful it is that we're all just temporary patterns of stardust arguing on the internet.

Right now, as you're reading this, billions of atoms that used to be inside exploding stars are arranged in the shape of you. Those same atoms have been everything from dinosaur bones to seawater to the ink in some medieval monk's manuscript. And tomorrow they'll be something else. Maybe part of a tree. Or a child's laugh. Or the rust on an old bridge in a city you'll never visit.

The universe spent 13.8 billion years setting up this exact moment where some pattern of consciousness called "you" and another called "me" get to have this weird little exchange. It's absurd. It's miraculous. Most of the time we forget how insane it is that anything exists at all.

I love that we're all just walking each other home for a little while.

Some days I feel like I'm mainlining the raw weirdness of existence. Like I'll be standing in a grocery store looking at a perfect avocado and suddenly get hit with this wave of "holy shit, none of this had to happen." Not the avocado, not consciousness, not love, not music, not the way dogs tilt their heads when they're confused. The fact that the universe bothered to become aware of itself at all feels like the biggest plot twist in cosmic history.

And we're all just... figuring it out. Making art. Falling in love. Getting our hearts broken. Eating tacos at 2am. Crying during songs. Building things that will outlast us. Forgetting where we put our keys.

I don't know. Maybe the meaning of life is just to notice how ridiculous and precious it all is while we have the chance. To stay curious. To stay soft. To keep being stupidly, stubbornly, beautifully human even though we know how the story ends.

Thanks for reading whatever this was. I hope today gave you at least one moment where you felt properly astonished to be alive.

The avocado section is particularly good this time of year.
